name: Amir ibn Muslim
title: Warrior of Basra
shortDescription: A sincere Shia from Basra who, along with his servant Salim, reached Mecca and accompanied Imam Hussain (AS) to Karbala.
fullStory:
Amir ibn Muslim al-Abdi was a prominent and sincere Shia in the city of Basra. When Imam Hussain (AS) was in Mecca and had not yet set out for Kufa, he sent a letter to the nobles of Basra.

Upon hearing this letter, Amir ibn Muslim immediately, along with his loyal servant (Salim) and a few other Shias from Basra, left the city at night and, after a long journey, joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan in Mecca.

Amir and his servant Salim accompanied the Imam throughout the journey from Mecca to Karbala. On the day of Ashura, with the start of Umar Sa'd's army's all-out attack (the first attack), Amir ibn Muslim drew his sword and manfully struck the heart of the enemy army.

Being one of the brave men of the Abd al-Qays tribe, after a heroic battle and taking down a number of Kufans, he was martyred alongside his loyal servant.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Amir ibn Muslim.
